Health Meets
Modern Glamour

A personal project designing a full multi-page e-commerce website for a fictional wellness brand — exploring how to make health products feel aspirational, accessible, and visually irresistible to a millennial audience.

Home, Shop, About
Figma
01
Project Overview

A Wellness Brand
Designed to Feel Good

Better Oodles is a personal design project — a fully realised fictional wellness brand targeting health-conscious millennials. The brief I set for myself was ambitious: design a complete, multi-page e-commerce website that makes health products feel as aspirational and desirable as luxury fashion, while remaining warm, accessible, and community-driven.

The wellness e-commerce space is saturated with two competing aesthetics — the clinical, sterile supplement site that communicates expertise but feels cold, and the maximalist, trend-chasing site that looks exciting but lacks trust. Better Oodles was an attempt to occupy the space between them: professional enough to be credible, warm enough to be welcoming, and bold enough to be memorable.

Scope of Work

02
The Brief & Target Audience

Who Is Better Oodles
Really For?

Wellness is no longer a niche interest — it's a mainstream lifestyle category. But most wellness brands still design as if their audience is either a committed athlete or a retiree taking vitamins. Better Oodles was conceived for the millennial in the middle: someone who cares about their health, wants products that work, but also wants the experience of buying them to feel elevated, not medicinal.

The Target User

  • Age 25–38 — digitally native, aesthetically aware, mobile-first shoppers
  • Health-conscious but not extreme — interested in sustainable wellness, not fad diets
  • Values community, transparency, and brands that feel like people, not corporations
  • Shops online instinctively — expects the UX to be as polished as the product
  • Influenced by visual platforms — the website needed to feel Instagram-worthy by default

Brand Pillars

  • Innovation & Technology — products that blend cutting-edge science with wellness principles
  • Accessibility & Practicality — health that fits into real life, not a strict routine
  • Community & Support — a brand that fosters connection, not just transactions
💡

Design challenge: Make health feel glamorous — without making glamour feel unattainable. Every design decision had to serve both the aspiration and the accessibility of the brand simultaneously.

"Wellness should feel like a treat, not a chore — and the website needed to make you feel that way before you'd read a single product description."

03
Page-by-Page Breakdown

Three Pages.
One Cohesive Story.

Better Oodles was designed as a complete website system — not a homepage with placeholder pages behind it. Each page has its own purpose, tone, and layout logic, while remaining unmistakably part of the same brand.

A
Homepage

The Emotional Entry Point

The homepage leads with feeling before function. The hero section uses a split-layout with lifestyle photography on the right — a woman exercising — that immediately communicates the brand's target audience and aspirational energy. The headline "Inspiring Health with Modern Glamour" sets the brand tone in seven words.

  • Three brand pillar cards with circular image headers break down the "why" before the "what"
  • An editorial About Us section with overlapping images creates visual depth and magazine-style layout
  • Product section uses clean card layout with wishlist icons — social commerce cues
  • Full-bleed Aristotle quote section builds emotional resonance before the footer
B
Shop Page

Where Browsing Becomes Buying

The shop page opens with a full-width hero featuring the flagship product — QuantumFlex Fitness Bands — with bold serif typography and a high-contrast "Buy now" CTA. This immediately communicates that Better Oodles sells premium, specific products — not a generic wellness catalogue.

  • Top Products 3-grid — curated featured items for first-time visitors
  • 4-column product grid — broader catalogue browse below the fold
  • Consistent card design with cart icon overlay and "Buy now" CTA on every product
  • Red footer creates a bold, unmistakable brand close — carries the brand colour into the final impression
C
About Page

The Brand Story, Told Visually

The About Us page uses an asymmetric image-and-text layout throughout — a deliberate departure from the standard centered text column. Overlapping photography creates a collage-like quality that feels editorial and premium, not templated.

  • Company History section with product and lifestyle photography sets the origin story in context
  • "Our Mission" section uses a right-aligned heading with a red text cursor accent — a small typographic detail that signals precision and brand craft
  • Team section with circular portrait photos adds the human layer that makes the brand feel trustworthy
  • Consistent blush background across all pages ties the system together at the page level
04
Key Design Decisions

The Choices That
Defined the Brand

Blush as the Base

The page background isn't white — it's a warm, dusty blush pink (#FAF0EF). This single decision changes the entire feel of the website. White feels clinical. Blush feels feminine, warm, and on-trend for the wellness and beauty-adjacent space. It also allows white product cards to lift off the background naturally without a heavy border or shadow.

Red as the Brand Voice

Crimson red appears in the logo, nav active states, headline accents, CTAs, and the entire footer — creating a recurring visual thread that ties every page together. Red in a wellness brand is an unusual choice (most go green or blue), which is precisely why it works: it gives Better Oodles a distinct, confident identity in a category full of sameness.

Italic Serif + Blue CTA

The logo and key headlines use an italic serif typeface that communicates elegance and warmth — softening the energy of the red. The nav CTA uses blue rather than red, creating a deliberate contrast that makes it more noticeable precisely because it breaks the page's colour pattern. Unexpected colour use draws the eye.

Overlapping Image Layouts

The About Us section and the homepage feature product and lifestyle images that overlap each other — a technique borrowed from editorial magazine design. This creates visual depth and makes the pages feel designed, not assembled. It requires more deliberate composition but the payoff is a brand that feels premium without using premium imagery.

The Red Footer

Every page closes with a full-width red footer. This isn't just a visual choice — it's a brand communication strategy. The footer becomes a signature closing statement: consistent, confident, unmistakably Better Oodles. It means every page ends on a strong brand note rather than fading into a grey or white footer that could belong to any website.

05
Colour & Visual Language

A Palette That
Feels Like the Brand

Brand Red
#E8192C

Logo, accents, footer, active states. Confident and distinctive in the wellness space.

Blush Base
#FAF0EF

Page backgrounds. Warm, feminine, wellness-adjacent — replaces clinical white.

CTA Blue
#1A56DB

Nav button, product buy CTAs. Breaks the red/blush pattern to draw the eye.

Near Black
#111111

Body text and headings. Warmer than pure black — pairs naturally with the blush base.

🎨

Colour tension is intentional: Red + blush creates warmth. Red + blue creates energy. The two CTAs (red accents vs. blue buttons) never compete because they perform different functions — brand voice vs. action prompt. Each colour has one job and does only that job.

06
Takeaways & Reflections

What Three Pages
Taught Me

Designing a complete multi-page website — even as a personal project — forces a level of design system thinking that a single-screen exercise never does. Every decision on the homepage had downstream consequences on the shop and about pages. This project significantly deepened how I think about design consistency as a form of brand communication.

🏗️

Design systems over pages

Designing three pages forces you to think in components, not compositions. The product card, the footer, the nav — each had to work in multiple contexts without breaking.

🎨

Background colour is a brand decision

Swapping white for blush was one decision that changed the entire feel of the brand. The background is the canvas — it shapes how every element on top of it is perceived.

📖

Editorial layouts build premium perception

Overlapping images and asymmetric text placement — borrowed from magazine design — made the brand feel premium without premium-budget photography.

🔴

Own your colour in the category

Red in wellness is unexpected. That's the point. The most memorable brands in any category tend to use the colour no one else in that category is using.

✍️

Copy is part of the design

"Inspiring Health with Modern Glamour" — seven words that do more brand positioning work than most visual elements on the page. Headline copy is a design decision.

🔚

End pages with intention

The red footer is a closing signature. Too many websites fade out — Better Oodles ends every page with the same energy it started with. Endings matter as much as openings.

"A personal project is where you get to answer the question: what would I build if I had no constraints at all? The answer is always revealing."

Better
Oodles
